title: Smart organic wines from A&D Wines, Vinho Verde, Portugal
summary: Website: https://andwines.pt/en/ This is a project based in the Baião subregion of Vinho Verde right next to the Douro. It
content:
Website: https://andwines.pt/en/

This is a project based in the Baião subregion of Vinho Verde right next to the Douro. It began with a family quinta Casa do Arrabalde (5 hectares of vines), and another property Quinta dos Espinhosos (7 hectares of vines) and this was then supplemented with the purchase of the much larger (33 hectares) Quinta de Santa Teresa.

They now have 45 hectares of vines all organically certified. Oenotourism is important here. All ferments are with indigenous yeasts.

Casa do Arrabalde 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
A field blend of Avesso, Alvarinho and Arinto from vineyards at 550 m. Fresh and supple with nice weight, and a strong mineral edge to the textured citrus fruit. Lively. 92/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Monólogo Santa Teresa Avesso 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Fresh, bright and linear with lovely weight and texture. Has direct citrus and pear fruit, and a nice crystalline quality. Very stylish. 93/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Monólogo Santa Teresa Arinto 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Fresh, bright and quite linear with lovely purity. Slightly salty lemony directness but also some nice texture. Lovely wine. 93/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Singular 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
This is a blend of Avesso, Arinto, Alvarinho and Malvasia Fina. 10% in oak. So textural and lively with bright lemony fruit and some ripe pear. Fresh and linear with a nice acid line. Pure with a wide dynamic range. 93/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Avesso 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Nice density with bright, pure citrus fruit. Good concentration, showing mineral-laced citrus and nice saline detail. Really expressive. 94/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Esculpido Avesso Vinhas Velhas 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. Old vines, 20% new oak. Powerful and layered with some spicy oak supporting the lively lemony fruit. Has freshness and concentration, with the oak adding an extra dimension. 92/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Rosé 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
This is 90% Touriga Nacional, 10% Vinhão. Textured and layered with sappy red cherry fruit and some strawberry. Bright but with good texture. Stylish. 92/100

Quinta de Santa Teresa Avesso Curtimenta 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Fermented on skins for two weeks. Bight, lively and expressive with citrus, peach and some zippy acidity. Good structure and nice brightness, showing lovely energy. 93/100

UK agent: Graft Wines

title: The wines of Son Antem Alaró, Mallorca, Spain (updated with new releases)
summary: Website: https://www.sonantemalaro.es/en/ Mallorca isn’t well known as a wine island, but there are some interesting projects here, and a recent
content:
Website: https://www.sonantemalaro.es/en/

Mallorca isn’t well known as a wine island, but there are some interesting projects here, and a recent addition to the fine wine scene here is the Son Antem project, which was started by a wine-importing couple in Sweden, Johan and Marin Lidby. I tried through the range and came away impressed.

Son Antem Alaró has 18 hectares of vines, all hand managed by the Lidby family.

Son Antem Malva 2024 Vino de la Tierra Mallorca, Spain
14% alcohol. This white is powerful, rich and intense with a lively acid line lying under the rich pear and peach fruit. The limestone gives the bracing acidity that provides a nice foil for the bold, slightly pithy fruit. Such nice intensity to this distinctive white wine, with a lively acid character driving things. 93/100

Son Antem Aura 2024 Vino de la Tierra Mallorca, Spain
13% alcohol. This white is made from the Giró Rus variety. Bold, rich and textural with some honey, nut and spice characters as well as some pear and peach. There’s innate richness, but it stays fresh and crystalline, with a touch of aniseed and some grainy detail. 92/100

Camins de Son Antem Callet 2024 Vino de la Tierra Mallorca, Spain
12.5% alcohol. This is a lighter-coloured red wine with beautiful aromatics of sweet strawberries and some glace cherry. In the mouth it is very textural with white pepper, cherry, strawberry and fine spicy notes. Lovely fine-grained texture here. Sleek and sweetly fruited with finesse. 94/100

Son Antem S’Era 2024 Vino de la Tierra Mallorca, Spain
12.5% alcohol. Relatively pale in colour, this is is juicy, fine and delicate. There’s a rich mid-palate core of sweet cherry and plum fruit with some damson bitterness, and also some dried herb character. The variety here is Callet, and it seems brilliantly adapted to this warm climate, with some rose, cherry, aniseed and mediterranean herb notes. It’s a little compressed on the finish, with some tannin and acid providing structure, but overall this is really compelling. 94/100

Camins de Son Antem Negre 2024 Vino de la Tierra Mallorca, Spain
14% alcohol. Manto Negro, Monastrell and Syrah. This is sleek, ripe and textured with appealing black cherry and berry fruits. There’s an innate richness and ripeness, but it’s hemmed in by a bit of grainy structure and just enough acidity. And the fruit character stays deliciously floral and fresh. 93/100

Older notes:

Son Antem Camins de Mallorca Giro Blanco 2023 Mallorca, Spain
12.5% alcohol. This is concentrated and grainy and salty with sweet, textured, ripe pear and citrus fruit (a touch of mandarin and lemon). This has such a lovely mineral flow on the palate with real finesse. Very expressive: ripe but well defined. 94/100

Son Antem Malva 2023 Mallorca
14% alcohol. This is really rich and expansive with sweet pear and table grape fruitiness, as well as some spicy highlights and a touch of saltiness. Rich, exuberant and really fruity, this has lots of personality, and good acidity balancing the sweet fruit. 91/100

Son Antem Camins de Mallorca Rouge 2022 Mallorca, Spain
12.5% alcohol. Lovely aromatics here with sleek cherries, raspberries and some aniseed, as well as a hint of pepper. The palate has a sweet fruit core with nice spicy detail. Pure and refined with warm Mediterranean herb notes but always so balanced with real finesse. Grainy and delicious. 93/100

Son Antem Alaró S’Era 2023 IGP Mallorca, Spain
12% alcohol. This is varietal Callet. Quite a pale cherry red colour, in the in-between land, not quite rosé, not quite red wine. Very fine and supple with sweet red cherry fruit and a silky elegance, and just a hint of structure. Has lovely texture and warmth with rounded sweet cherry fruit. 94/100

Son Antem Vespre 2022 Mallorca, Spain
12.5% alcohol. This is supple, expressive and elegant with real elegance, and a sweet, textured mid-palate with stony cherries and plums, and then notes of herbs, pepper and fine spices framing the fruit on the finish. This is very fine: restrained, sappy and showing warm-climate elegance. 94/100

Son Antem Sa Tanca 2022 Mallorca, Spain
13% alcohol. This is Manto Negro, from iron-rich stony soils. Beautiful aromatics: sweet cherry and raspberry fruit with some nice spicy detail and a beautiful flow across the palate. Ripe but really elegance with great purity. 95/100

title: Highlights: Louis Jadot Famille Gagey Bouzeron 2023
summary: Website: https://www.louisjadot.com/bo/wp-content/pdf/vins/vin-1325-bouzeron.pdf Previously labelled as Domaine Gagey, this is now Famille Gagey, presumably because some non-domaine fruit enters the blend.
content:
Website: https://www.louisjadot.com/bo/wp-content/pdf/vins/vin-1325-bouzeron.pdf

Previously labelled as Domaine Gagey, this is now Famille Gagey, presumably because some non-domaine fruit enters the blend. Bouzeron is a small appellation in Bourgogne of 47 hectares, devoted solely to Aligoté. This is a superb example of what this variety can do, and it’s vinified two-thirds in tank and then one-third in barrels, of which 20% are new.

Louis Jadot Famille Gagey Bouzeron 2023 Bourgogne, France
This is a really good Aligoté. Lovely aromatics with lively citrus notes as well as ripe pear. The palate is fine grained and textural with lovely depth of flavour, and notes of pear skin, crystalline lemons and a hint of apricot. So lovely, showing real depth, and a slight saltiness on the finish. 94/100

title: How pruning is done in Bourgogne (Burgundy)
summary: I was recently filming in Bourgogne (Burgundy), and there were lots of gaps while cameras and lighting were being set
content:
I was recently filming in Bourgogne (Burgundy), and there were lots of gaps while cameras and lighting were being set up. So I went to look at the vines. It was early April and the buds had burst and the leaves were coming out. It’s a good time to see the way the vines are pruned. Here are a bunch of pictures showing some of the variation, almost all taken in Grand Cru Corton Charlemagne vineyards.

Viticulture is quite uniform here. Vines are planted at high density, with 10 000 of them per hectare, and 1 x 1 m spacing. The fruiting wire is low at around 30 cm. The vines are single-Guyot pruned, with a short cane of 6-8 buds, and a two bud replacement spur if there’s one to be found. Each vine is only being asked to ripen a small quantity of grapes because of the close spacing. Canopies aren’t high and because of the spacing, over-row tractors are used, stradding the row they are treating.

I saw very little herbicide use: these are expensive vineyards, and almost everyone works the soil. In less fancy vineyards, the situation might be different. Once you leave the more celebrated appellations vineyards can look quite different in terms of spacing and pruning (for example, the Hautes Côtes).

title: Invincible, a new Douro star winery from Marc Kent and Rita Marques
summary: Website: https://invincible.com.pt/ This is the Douro project of Marc Kent and Rita Marques. Marc is well known as the founder
content:
Website: https://invincible.com.pt/

This is the Douro project of Marc Kent and Rita Marques. Marc is well known as the founder and head of Boekenhoutskloof in South Africa, but he’s fallen in love with the Douro and now spends most of his time there. Rita previously made wines under the Conceito label, including a fabulous Bastardo that I was in love with!

Invincible (full name Companhia de Vinhos Invencível) began in 2000, so it’s still early days for this project, and the top wines are only being released now. The winery is based in the Cima Corgo, right in the heart of the Douro.

These wines range from everyday affordable (the Numero Um is around £12 in the UK, and is available in supermarkets) to more ambitious. They also make a Vinho Verde called Natural Mystic, which retails for £16.

Invincible Número Um Branco 2025 Douro, Portugal (sample)
A little bit of Muscat is used here, fermented in amphora. The resy of the wine is in concrete. Bright and stony with lovely pure fruit. Crisp with nice balance, and real potential. 91/100

Invincible Número Dois Branco 2024 Douro, Portugal
Made from older vineyards, this is half concrete and half barrel. Some oak adds an extra dimension. Bright and linear, this is fresh with some nutty, mealy hints, with nice balance. 91/100

Invincible ‘The Invincible’ Branco 2024 Douro, Portugal
This is first release. Juicy and bright, this is a vivid white wine with nice intensity. Zippy with some stony minerality and a touch of spicy oak. Very sophisticated. 93/100

Invincible Número Um Tinto 2024 Douro, Portugal
Juicy, bright and fruity with a nice focus to the sweet raspberry and cherry fruit. Vital. Lovely fruit with liqourice and a touch of pepper. 92/100 (this is £12.75 in Sainsbury’s in the UK.)

Invincible Número Dois Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
60 year old vines, fermented in concrete tanks, then spends one year in barrel and another year in concrete tank. Juicy, bright and vivid with nice spicy framing to the fresh cherry and berry fruits, with a lick of liquorice. Modern in style showing bright fruit, with some tarriness around the margins. 92/100

Invincible ‘The Invincible’ Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
From a vineyard planted in 1930. Concentrated but supple with lovely pure black cherry and blackberry fruit with a nice salty edge and some good tannic structure. Very fine and expressive. 94/100

Natural Mystic Vinhas Velhas 2025 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Mostly Loureiro. Lively, vivid and bright with nice focus and fruitiness. Very zippy but has texture and balance. 92/100

title: Brindiwell, a new producer in Devon, England
summary: Website: https://www.brindiwell.co.uk/pages/about-us I first met James Matyear when he was managing the vineyard at Black Chalk. But with his wife
content:
Website: https://www.brindiwell.co.uk/pages/about-us

I first met James Matyear when he was managing the vineyard at Black Chalk. But with his wife Jo he’s now got his own project. Back in 2016 they found their site, and after diligent preparation, planted in 2018. Viticulture is organic less the certification, with no herbicides used. They’ve also planted 1000 native trees on the site, including 600 oak trees. This is very cool.

Brindiwell Blanc de Noirs 2022 Devon, England
12.2% alcohol. 26 months on lees, with 9% of the base wine aged in barrel. This is fresh and aromatic with lovely pure cherry and lemon fruit, showing nice delicacy and texture. Fruit-driven with real finesse and purity, and a hint of sherbetty brightness on the finish. The balance here is superb: it’s fruit dominant, but not techno, with some personality. This is the combination of a really good site, good viticulture and sensitive winemaking. And it’s just the first release. 92/100

Brindiwell Pinot Noir Rosé 2023 Devon, England
12.6% alcohol. Clones 114, 115 and 777, stainless steel ferment and ageing, 25% malolactic fermentation. Pale in colour, this is a really fine, delicate, textural rose with sweet red cherry fruit, a touch of redcurrant and raspberry, with some fine sappy, leafy hints in the background. There’s a nice sleek quality to the fruit, and it finishes with a fine crystalline spicy quality. This is really good. 92/100

title: Video: how sparkling wine is made – the final stage, disgorging by hand
summary: The last stage in making sparkling wine is called disgorgement. The second fermentation has taken place in the bottle and
content:
The last stage in making sparkling wine is called disgorgement. The second fermentation has taken place in the bottle and it leaves a sediment of dead yeast cells and tartrate crystals.

So the first stage is to settle these at the end of the bottle neck by a process called riddling. Then the bottle neck is plunged into a freezing glycol solution in order to freeze it, ready for the next stage when the crown cap is released and the pressure expels this plug, leaving clear wine.

A small sugar addition, called dosage, follows (this is dissolved in wine) and then the bottle is topped up with the same sparkling wine. A cork is inserted and then the metal cap and wire twist (the plaque and muselet) are added to hold it in place. Job done.

This is the process done by hand at Colonjes winery in The Netherlands. It’s also possible to automate the job for larger production runs.

title: Video: Vitibot Bakus, a vineyard robot, in action in Bourgogne
summary: I’ve known about Vitibot Bakus, an autonomous vineyard robot, for a few years, but this week I saw it in
content:
I’ve known about Vitibot Bakus, an autonomous vineyard robot, for a few years, but this week I saw it in action for the first time. It was in the Grand Cru Corton-Charlemagne vineyards of Domaine Louis Latour, and they are very happy with the way it works. They are using it for cultivation under-vine, a time-consuming task that needs a degree of precision. The design of the Bakus means it can turn around in narrow headlands very effectively.

Here’s a film of it in action:

You can see it working in more detail here: https://vitibot.fr/?lang=en

title: Quinta de la Rosa: the elegant side of Portugal’s Douro
summary: Website: https://www.quintadelarosa.com/ I’ve been following the wines of Quinta de La Rosa for a long time. Owned by the Bergqvist
content:
Website: https://www.quintadelarosa.com/

I’ve been following the wines of Quinta de La Rosa for a long time. Owned by the Bergqvist family, it’s a beautifully situated wine farm situated on the banks of the Douro just above Pinhão.

I’ve written about the Quinta extensively here. The modern story of the Quinta begins in 1988 when Tim Bergvist and his daughter Sophia (the current proprietor) decided to bring wines and Ports to market under the name of La Rosa. Things moved forwards fast when they hired winemaker Jorge Moreira (who also makes wines under his Poeira brand), and the wines have kept getting better. They also bought and planted a second quinta in the Douro Superior in 2006.

Jorge says that they are trying to change the philosophy of the La Rosa wines, for example by planting a new 10 hectare plot on a north-facing site. The whites, planted higher, are being made in a mineral, austere style, while the red grapes such as Baga, Touriga Francesca and Alfrocheiro are helping to make reds that are lighter with more tension. It’s working.

THE WINES

Quinta de la Rosa Branco 2024 Douro, Portugal
Juicy and bright with some citrus drive, as well as some subtle richer notes and a bit of pithy detail. Lively, focused and quite lovely. 90/100

Quinta de la Rosa Reserva Branco 2024 Douro, Portugal
This is a typical Douro white blend but with a touch of Alvarinho, too, aged for 6 months in used barrels. Juicy and lively with nice energy, and a pithy twist. Lovely purity with nice texture and poise. 92/100

Quinta de la Rosa Gouveio 2023 Douro, Portugal
First release of this wine (although it was first made in 2021). Gouveio in a gully, north-facing, replanted in 2019. It’s a 10 hectare plot. Tight and focused with nice texture and weight. A minerally, grainy style. 94/100

Quinta de la Rosa Cerejinha 2023 Douro, Portugal
This was first released in 2021, and it’s a blend of Alvarinho (with some skins) and Arinto. Lively and fruity with nice texture and a bit of structure. Fine citrus fruit with some grip, and pronounced acidity keeping everything alive. Pear and grapefruit notes. 94/100

Quinta de la Rosa ‘Tim’ Grande Reserva 2023 Douro, Portugal
Jorge says he goes for acid and reduction in this wine, with quite a bit of Arinto in the blend. Complex and layered with nice depth, showing powerful pear and peach fruit with some lemons. Fresh and expressive with a good acid line. Combines freshness and depth. 95/100

Quinta de la Rosa Passagem Branco 2024 Douro, Portugal
This comes from the Douro Superior property, Bandeiras. They have replanted some buts at the top in 2019 (changing Rabigato for Arinto). Fermented and aged in stainless steel. Linear and focused with nice citrus and pear fruit, as well as hints of grapefruit. Nice acid line here. 92/100

Quinta de la Rosa Passagem Branco Reserva 2024 Douro, Portugal
A bit of skin contact for this, as well as a good dose of Rabigato, used barrels. Fresh, stony and nicely mineral with some depth to the citrus fruit. Quite textural with some sophistication. 93/100

Quinta de la Rosa Leve 2024 Douro, Portugal
This red is made from Tinta Roriz, Tinto Cão and a bit of Baga, picked early. Fresh, bright and stony with red cherries and plums as well as a hint of strawberry. Nice sappiness. This is really nicely balanced, and is juicy with a bit of grip. Massive drinkability. 92/100

Quinta de la Rosa DuoRosa 2023 Douro, Portugal
Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca and Tinta Roriz. Aiming at freshness. Salty, slightly fishy edge to the bright berry fruits, with a touch of cherry. Bright and grippy with nice balance. Delicious. 92/100

Quinta de la Rosa Reserva Tinto 2022 Douro, Portugal
Touriga Nacional and Tinta Roriz. Lovely weight here. This is supple and elegant but also has nice fine-grained structure. This shows some nice spicy detail. Very expressive and drinkable, but it also has nice weight. 94/100

Quinta de la Rosa Tinto Cão 2021 Douro, Portugal
First made in 2019, and then again in this vintage. Bright, lively, stony and mineral with good structure under the supple cherry and berry fruit, with a bit of grip. Stylish and expressive with good poise. 93/100

Quinta de la Rosa Grande Reserva Tinto 2021 Douro, Portugal
Touriga Nacional and Touriga Franca, 18 months in oak. Nice concentration and balance with a savoury, stony edge to the blackberry and black cherry fruit. Fine-grained with nice refinement and some supporting oak. Classical style. 94/100

Quinta de la Rosa Vinha Vale do Inferno 2021 Douro, Portugal
Touriga Nacional from a remarkable plot with elaborate terracing. Lovely balance here: aromatic and refined with black cherries, spice and a hint of tar. So classy with everything in balance. Mineral, stony and expressive. 95/100

Quinta de la Rosa Passagem Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Touriga Nacional and Touriga Franca. Highly aromatic with tar, spice and floral black cherries. The palate is supple with lovely sweet cherry and raspberry fruit with nice detail. Lovely balance here: fruity and delicious. 93/100

Quinta de la Rosa Passagem Reserva Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Juicy and supple, this is vivid and floral with lovely energy to the bright cherry and plum fruit with a hint of pepper and tar. So expressive with nice ripe fruit and good acidity. 94/100

Quinta de la Rosa Passagem Grande Reserva Tinto 2027 Douro, Portugal
Minty edge to the bright cherry and plum fruit with a hint of earth and spice. Evolving nicely, with a grippy finish. 93/100

title: Impressive Dão wines from Julia Kemper
summary: There’s something special about Portugal’s Dão wine region, and there’s now a roll call of producers doing really good things
content:
There’s something special about Portugal’s Dão wine region, and there’s now a roll call of producers doing really good things here. Among them is Julia Kemper.

Her family (the Melos) were winegrowers, while she pursued a career as a lawyer in Lisbon. In 2000 Julia inherited her father’s vines, she began farming organically in 2003, and in 2008 began releasing wines under her own name.

The family farm is Quinta do Cruzeiro, and altitude here hits 700 m. The 15 hectares of vines were all replanted in 2003.

There have been some changes here. Julia has changed the labels and rethought the entire range. These changes came in two years ago.

Four years ago they changed viticulturist and have been trying to work better on this side of things. They are more precise with their picks (which span August to October), working with the resident enologist Nuno Bastos. This is his first job outside Bairrada, where he was a sparkling specialist.

THE WINES

Julia Kemper 2CV Branco 2023 Dão, Portugal
2CV is a new scaleable brand, and though this vintage is all from organic fruit, this might not be the case going forwards. This is an inexpensive wine (retail €7.50) named after the Citroen 2CV: the Portuguese factory making this car was near where the winery is. It’s fresh, bright and light with nice easy fruit, finishing fresh. 88/100

Julia Kemper Branco 2023 Dão, Portugal
60% Malvasia Fina and 40% Encruzado, in seasoned oak. Nice presence and brightness here with pure citrus fruit, a touch of pear and a lovely fruit quality. Juicy with nice energy. 91/100

Julia Kemper Encruzado 2024 Dão, Portugal
No oak here, and launched young. Precise, linear and bright with lovely citrus drive and good acidity. Very fine and pure, focused, with good energy. 92/100

Julia Kemper Reserva Branco 2021 Dão, Portugal
Encruzado that spends 14 months in used French oak. Stony with nice weight and a savoury edge. Mineral with some spicy oak character in the background. Showing some appealing savoury evolution. 92/100

Julia Kemper 2CV Rosé 2024 Dão, Portugal
Tinta Roriz and Jaen. Bright, lively and zippy with nice acidity. Pure with some stony mineral notes. Lively style. 87/100

Julia Kemper Elpenor Palhete 2024 Dão, Portugal
This is Alfrocheiro, Jaen and 10% Encruzado (white grapes). Pale cherry red in colour. Juicy, supple and leafy with nice brightness and good acidity. Sappy and delicious. 92/100

Julia Kemper Elpenor Lagareta 2023 Dão, Portugal
12% alcohol. 60% Touriga Nacional, 40% Alfrocheiro. These are foot trodden, macerated for a few hours and then pressed. Lovely floral, spicy nose with some pepper notes. Juicy and vivid with nice structure and lovely pretty fruit. Very fresh and fine, with a bit of grip on the finish. Brilliant. 94/100

Julia Kemper 2CV Tinto NV Dão, Portugal
This is a blend of wines from 2017/18/19. Juicy and spicy with nice mellow structure, showing cherries and plums with a bit of spice and some easiness on the finish. 89/100

Julia Kemper Tinto 2019 Dão, Portugal
Touriga Nacional, Jaen and Alfrocheiro, no oak. Nice depth to the smooth, sweet cherry and plum fruit with some spicy underpinnings, and a hint of tar on the finish. 91/100

Julia Kemper Reserva Tinto 2017 Dão, Portugal
50% Touriga Nacional (matured in cement tanks) plus Jaen and Alfrocheiro, matured in used oak. Has a small amount of older wine in the blend, too. Nice freshness but also some depth. Spicy and mellow with notes of tar and spice. Has a dry, slightly bitter finish or it would have merited a higher rating. 91/100

Julia Kemper Touriga Nacional 2013 Dão, Portugal
This comes from a vineyard at 700 m, and it’s matured in used oak. Spicy and earthy, with mellow fruit and a touch of elegance. Nice depth here. Smooth and refined, this has aged well. 92/100
